prs.title: fix(react-start): align server-entry d.ts output with exports map
prs.body:
## Related Issue
Closes #7038

## Summary
- Fixes the `@tanstack/react-start/server-entry` declaration output path regression introduced with TS6 build config changes
- Adds a dedicated server-entry tsconfig with `rootDir: ./src/default-entry`
- Wires `vite.config.server-entry.ts` to that tsconfig so emitted declarations match package exports

## Root Cause
The server-entry build did not use a tsconfig with the correct `rootDir`, so declarations were emitted at:
- `dist/default-entry/esm/src/default-entry/server.d.ts` (actual)
instead of:
- `dist/default-entry/esm/server.d.ts` (export-mapped expected path)

## Validation
- `pnpm --filter @tanstack/react-start run clean && pnpm --filter @tanstack/react-start run build`
- Confirmed `dist/default-entry/esm/server.d.ts` exists
- Confirmed nested `dist/default-entry/esm/src/default-entry/server.d.ts` is no longer emitted

prs.title: docs: clarify nitro auto-imports with compatibilityVersion 5
prs.body:
### 🔗 Linked issue

Closes #34641

### 📚 Description

Document Nitro auto-import behavior when using `future.compatibilityVersion: 5`.

This adds migration guidance that Nitro auto-imports are disabled by default with compatibility v5 and explains two options:
- enable `experimental.nitroAutoImports: true`
- add explicit imports from `nitro/h3`

prs.title: feat(backend): enhance error logging in ComponentDatabaseHandler.getC…

labels.name: needs general test
labels.description: This is general testing, meaning that there is no org specific issue to check for

prs.body:
**Changes:**
- Add try-catch blocks to capture all exceptions in getComponent method
- Log component ID and error details for better traceability
- Log specific error when component is not found (404)
- Include full stack trace for unexpected exceptions
- Maintain existing exception throwing behavior

Resolves #3010

[//]: # (This program and the accompanying materials are made)
[//]: # (available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0)
[//]: # (which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/)
[//]: # (SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0)

## Summary

This PR enhances error logging in the `ComponentDatabaseHandler.getComponent()` method to improve debugging and observability when issues occur during component retrieval.

The current implementation throws exceptions when errors occur but doesn't log detailed information about the exception, making it difficult for developers and maintainers to debug issues.

**Issue**: #3010 - Enhance Error Logging in ComponentDatabaseHandler.getComponent

**Dependencies**: No new dependencies added. Uses existing SLF4J logger already present in the class.

### Suggest Reviewer
@GMishx @deo002

### How To Test?

The changes add logging only and do not modify the functional behavior. Testing can be done by:

1. **Component not found (404)**: Call `getComponent()` with invalid component ID. Expected: Error log shows "Component not found in database. Component ID: {id}"

2. **Database error**: Simulate database connectivity issue. Expected: Error log shows component ID and exception details with stack trace

3. **Successful retrieval**: Call with valid component ID and user. Expected: No error logs, component returned successfully (existing behavior unchanged)

### Checklist
Must:
- [x] All related issues are referenced in commit messages and in PR
- [x] No new dependencies added
- [x] Changes follow existing logging patterns in the project
- [x] No functional changes, only observability improvements

prs.title: feat(rest): enhance licenses API with missing fields and quick filter
prs.body:
Add shortname field to license response and implement quick filter functionality to improve API usability and search capabilities.

**Changes:**
- Include shortname in convertToEmbeddedLicense method
- Add luceneSearch parameter for license type filtering
- Support searchText parameter for quick filtering

Resolves #3407

[//]: # (This program and the accompanying materials are made)
[//]: # (available under the terms of the Eclipse Public License 2.0)
[//]: # (which is available at https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/)
[//]: # (SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0)

## Summary

This PR addresses issue #3407 by adding two missing features to the `/licenses` API:

1. **Missing Fields**: The `shortname` field was not being returned in license responses, even though it exists in the License model. This has been fixed by updating the `convertToEmbeddedLicense()` method in `RestControllerHelper`.

2. **Quick Filter Support**: Implemented lucene search capability for filtering licenses by license type, following the same pattern used in releases, projects, and components APIs.

**Issue**: #3407 - Missing fields in /licenses API and Quick Filter support

**Dependencies**: No new dependencies added. This PR uses existing service methods (`quickSearchLicenseType`) and follows established patterns.

### Suggest Reviewer
@deo002 @akshitjoshii @GMishx

### How To Test?

**Test 1: Verify shortname field is returned**
```bash
GET /api/licenses
```
Expected: Response now includes `shortname` field for each license

**Test 2: Quick filter without luceneSearch**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?page=0&size=20
```
Expected: Returns all licenses (existing behavior unchanged)

**Test 3: Quick filter with luceneSearch**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?luceneSearch=true&searchText=Apache
```
Expected: Returns only licenses with license type matching "Apache"

**Test 4: luceneSearch with empty searchText**
```bash
GET /api/licenses?luceneSearch=true
```
Expected: Returns all licenses (fallback to default behavior)

### Checklist
Must:
- [x] All related issues are referenced in commit messages and in PR
- [x] Changes follow existing code patterns in the project
- [x] No new compilation errors introduced
- [x] Pre-commit hooks passed

## Summary of Changes

This PR adds an optional `attachmentId` query parameter to the existing `/releases/{id}/licenseFileList` endpoint, enabling users to fetch the license file list for a specific attachment instead of only from the approved CLI attachment.

### Issue Reference
**Fixes #3472** - Need Endpoint to Fetch License File List of an Attachment of a Release

This solves the issue by:
- Modifying the existing `/releases/{id}/licenseFileList` endpoint to accept an optional `attachmentId` query parameter
- When `attachmentId` is provided, the endpoint returns the license file list for that specific attachment
- When `attachmentId` is NOT provided, the endpoint maintains the original behavior (returns license file list from approved CLI attachment)
- This enables the UI to show the license file list modal when clicking the info badge in the Release clearing details SPDX attachments table

### Dependencies
**No new dependencies added.** This change only modifies existing code to add the optional parameter.

### Changes Made
- **ReleaseController.java**: Added `attachmentId` as optional `@RequestParam` and updated OpenAPI documentation
- **Sw360ReleaseService.java**: Updated `getReleaseLicenseFileListInfo()` method to filter by specific attachment when `attachmentId` is provided

## Suggest Reviewer
@deo002 - Original issue author who can verify this implementation meets the requirements for the Release clearing details SPDX attachments table functionality

## How To Test?

### Testing Steps
1. **Test backward compatibility (without attachmentId parameter)**:
   ```bash
   GET /api/releases/{validReleaseId}/licenseFileList

prs.title: fix: enable decorations for structure: inline
prs.body:
- Invoke code transformer hooks for inline structures - Build synthetic code element to allow transformers like decorations to work - Add tests for single-line and multi-line decorations with inline structure Fixes #992 ### Description This PR fixes an issue where decorations don't work with `structure: inline` because the `transformerDecorations` transformer's `code` hook was not being invoked for inline structures. **What changed:** - Modified `tokensToHast` in `code-to-hast.ts` to invoke the `code` transformer hook for inline structures - For inline mode, a synthetic `` element is built from the root's children, transformers are applied, and the result is extracted back to inline format - This allows transformers like `transformerDecorations` to work correctly with inline structures while maintaining compatibility with all existing transformers **Implementation approach:** 1. Collect inline tokens and build a temporary line-based structure 2. Apply all `code` hook transformers (including decorations) 3. Flatten the structure back to inline format with `
` separators ### Linked Issues Fixes #992 ### Additional context - Added 2 new test cases with snapshots to verify single-line and multi-line decorations work correctly with `structure: inline` - All existing tests pass - The fix maintains backward compatibility - no breaking changes to the API or existing behavior

prs.title: feat: Add double arrow arrowhead option
prs.body:
## What

Adds a new double-sided arrow arrowhead option to the arrowhead picker, addressing the feature request in #10288.

## Why

Users requested the ability to have double-sided arrows (arrows with a middle line between the two outer arrow lines) for better visual representation in diagrams, especially for showing bidirectional relationships or enhanced emphasis.

## How

- Added `arrow_double` to the `Arrowhead` type definition
- Implemented rendering logic that draws three lines (two outer arrow lines + one middle line)
- Created the `ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon` component for the UI
- Added the option to the arrowhead picker with keyboard shortcut `t`
- Added English localization string

## Changes

**Type definitions:**
- `packages/element/src/types.ts` - Added `arrow_double` to Arrowhead union type

**Rendering:**
- `packages/element/src/bounds.ts` - Added size and angle handling for arrow_double
- `packages/element/src/shape.ts` - Implemented three-line rendering (outer two + middle)

**UI:**
- `packages/excalidraw/components/icons.tsx` - Created ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon component
- `packages/excalidraw/actions/actionProperties.tsx` - Added to arrowhead options with 't' shortcut

**Localization:**
- `packages/excalidraw/locales/en.json` - Added "arrowhead_arrow_double": "Double arrow"

## Testing

- ✅ Type checking passes
- ✅ No compilation errors
- ✅ Follows existing code patterns for arrowhead types
- ✅ Works with all arrow types (sharp, round, elbow)
- ✅ RTL support included

Closes #10288

prs.title: fix: re-export Duration type in intervalToDuration to fix TypeDoc documentation (#4059)
prs.body:
### Description

Fixes #4059 

This PR addresses the issue where the `Duration` interface documentation was not appearing in the TypeDoc-generated documentation when accessed through the `intervalToDuration` function page.

### Problem

When navigating to the Duration type from the `intervalToDuration` function documentation (https://date-fns.org/v4.1.0/docs/intervalToDuration#types/Duration/2523), the documentation page showed "No documentation available" despite the interface having proper JSDoc comments in `src/types.ts`.

### Solution

Added an explicit re-export of the `Duration` type in `src/intervalToDuration/index.ts`:

```typescript
// Re-export Duration type to ensure TypeDoc picks up its documentation
export type { Duration } from "../types.ts";

prs.title: Day 3: C++ heap-based solution for Trapping Rain Water II
prs.body:
Hi! 👋

I'd like to contribute a C++ solution for Day 3's problem. 

My approach uses a heap-based algorithm with a priority queue and custom comparator. It processes cells from the boundary inward, starting with the lowest heights first, which helps efficiently calculate the trapped water.

The solution follows the contribution guidelines:
- One C++ solution per user
- Clean, readable code
- Different approach from existing solutions

Thanks for organizing this! Looking forward to contributing more. 🚀

prs.title: Add iterative C++ solution for Day 2 LeetCode problem
prs.body:
### 🚀 What does this PR do?
- Adds solution for LeetCode Daily Challenge on Day 2 (October 2, 2025) in C++.
- Implements an iterative approach to solve the "Maximum Number of Bottles You Can Drink" problem where the exchange rate increases after each exchange.

### 📝 Issue Reference:
Fixes issue: #2 (Day 2 LeetCode Daily Challenge)

### 🔍 Checklist:
Please ensure the following before submitting your PR:
- [x] I have placed my solution in the correct folder (`solutions/day02/`).
- [x] My code follows clean coding practices and is efficient.
- [x] I have added comments in my code to explain the approach.
- [ ] I have updated the `README.md` for the specific day with a brief explanation of my solution.
- [x] I have tested the solution on LeetCode and it works correctly.

### 🔧 Solution Details:
- **Language Used**: C++
- **Time Complexity**: O(√n) where n is the initial number of bottles
- **Space Complexity**: O(1) - constant space usage

### 🌟 Additional Notes (if any):
This iterative solution complements the existing recursive and mathematical approaches in the day02 folder. The algorithm efficiently handles the increasing exchange rate by tracking the current number of bottles and incrementing both the exchange rate and answer count in each iteration. The solution stops when we don't have enough bottles to make another exchange, ensuring optimal performance without recursion overhead.

prs.title: 🚀 Add Enhanced Space Shooter Game for Hacktoberfest
prs.body:
# 🚀 Enhanced Space Shooter Game - Hacktoberfest 2025 Contribution

## 🎮 Game Overview
A modern, feature-rich space shooter game built with pure HTML5 Canvas and JavaScript. This game offers progressive difficulty, multiple enemy types, and engaging mechanics that make it a perfect addition to the A2 Games collection.

## ✨ Features:
- Progressive wave system with increasing difficulty
- 4 different enemy types with unique behaviors
- Enemy shooting mechanics starting from wave 2
- Full health restoration between waves
- Smooth HTML5 Canvas graphics with particle effects
- Responsive controls (WASD + Space)
- Modern UI with Orbitron font and neon effects
- Real-time score and health tracking
- Proper wave completion system

## 🛸 Enemy Types:
- **Basic Enemies** (Red Rectangles) - Simple downward movement
- **Shooter Enemies** (Orange Diamonds) - Fire bullets at player from wave 2+
- **Fast Enemies** (Purple Triangles) - Quick and agile movement from wave 3+
- **Heavy Enemies** (Green Squares) - Multiple hit points and stronger attacks from wave 4+

## 🎯 Gameplay Mechanics:
- **Wave System**: Each wave requires defeating 5+ enemies to progress
- **Health System**: Start with 100 health, get damaged by enemy contact/bullets
- **Scoring**: Earn points for destroying enemies
- **Progressive Difficulty**: New enemy types unlock each wave
- **Recovery**: Full health restored between waves

## 🔧 Technical Features:
- Clean, comment-free code
- Responsive canvas that adapts to container size
- Smooth 60fps gameplay with requestAnimationFrame
- Particle explosion effects
- Collision detection system
- Modern ES6+ JavaScript

## 🌟 Open Source Contribution
Built as a quality contribution to the open-source gaming community during Hacktoberfest 2025.

## 🚀 How to Play:
1. Use WASD keys to move your spaceship
2. Press Spacebar to shoot enemies
3. Avoid enemy bullets and collisions
4. Survive waves to achieve high scores
5. Each wave gets progressively harder!

Ready to defend Earth from the alien invasion! 🛸👾

prs.title: fix: prevent duplicate customer group prices causing 500 error

labels.name: Change Request
labels.description: if need any changes on PR

prs.body:
- Add validation in ProductCustomerGroupPriceRepository to check for duplicate customer group + quantity combinations
- Add client-side validation in Vue component to prevent invalid submissions
- Add user-friendly error message translation
- Convert database constraint violations to proper validation exceptions

Fixes: Internal Server Error when adding duplicate group discounts for same customer group and quantity
Resolves: #10976

## Issue Reference
#10976

## Description
This PR fixes the Internal Server Error (500) that occurs when admin tries to apply a discount for the same customer group more than once on a product with the same quantity.

**Root Cause:** The system was throwing a database unique constraint violation (`SQLSTATE[23000]: Integrity constraint violation: 1062 Duplicate entry`) instead of proper validation.

**Solution:**
1. **Backend Validation**: Added duplicate checking in `ProductCustomerGroupPriceRepository` before database insertion
2. **Frontend Validation**: Added client-side validation in Vue.js component to prevent invalid submissions  
3. **Error Handling**: Convert database constraint violations to user-friendly validation messages
4. **Translation**: Added proper error message for duplicate entries

**Files Changed:**
- `packages/Webkul/Product/src/Repositories/ProductCustomerGroupPriceRepository.php` - Added validation logic
- `packages/Webkul/Admin/src/Resources/views/catalog/products/edit/price/group.blade.php` - Added client-side validation
- `packages/Webkul/Admin/src/Resources/lang/en/app.php` - Added error message translation

## How To Test This?
1. Login as Admin
2. Navigate to Catalog → Products → Edit any product → Group Discounts
3. Create a discount for a customer group (e.g., "Guest") with quantity 2
4. Try to add another discount for the same customer group with the same quantity 2
5. **Before Fix**: System throws 500 Internal Server Error
6. **After Fix**: System shows validation message "A discount for this customer group with the same quantity already exists."

**Additional Test Cases:**
- Same customer group with different quantities → Should work ✅
- Different customer groups with same quantity → Should work ✅
- Valid single entries → Should work as before ✅

## Documentation
- [ ] My pull request requires an update on the documentation repository.

## Branch Selection
- [x] Target Branch: 2.3

## Tailwind Reordering
Not applicable - no Tailwind classes modified.

prs.title: fix(shared): correct email provider documentation URLs - fixes #9251
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes the incorrect Amazon SES documentation URL and other email provider URLs in the Novu app as reported in issue #9251.

## Problem
Users clicking on documentation links from the Amazon SES integration (and other email providers) in the Novu app were encountering 404 errors because the URLs pointed to an incorrect path structure.

## Solution
Updated all email provider documentation URLs from the incorrect structure:
- **Before:** `https://docs.novu.co/integrations/providers/email/{provider}`  
- **After:** `https://docs.novu.co/platform/integrations/email/{provider}`

## Changes Made

### Files Modified:
- `packages/shared/src/consts/providers/channels/email.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/consts/providers/configurations/provider-configuration.ts`

### Providers Updated (16 total):
- Amazon SES (main issue reported)
- Mailgun, Mailjet, Mailtrap, Mandrill
- Postmark, SendGrid, Sendinblue (Brevo)
- NetCore, Custom SMTP, MailerSend
- Outlook365, Infobip, Resend, Plunk, SparkPost

### Manual Setup URLs:
Also fixed manual setup guide URLs for providers with configuration options:
- Amazon SES, SendGrid, Resend, Mailgun

## Impact
- ✅ Users will no longer encounter 404 errors when clicking documentation links
- ✅ All email provider documentation links now point to the correct URL structure  
- ✅ Ensures consistency across all email provider integrations
- ✅ Improves user experience when setting up email integrations

## Testing
- [x] Verified all URLs follow the correct `/platform/integrations/email/` structure
- [x] Confirmed no TypeScript compilation errors
- [x] All email providers now have consistent documentation links

Closes #9251

prs.title: Fix flaky test_download_with_proxy_https_timeout
prs.body:
Fixes #7060

## Problem
The test `test_download_with_proxy_https_timeout` was failing intermittently with `OpenSSL.SSL.Error` (wrapped in `ResponseNeverReceived`) instead of the expected `TimeoutError`. This happened due to SSL handshake timing issues during proxy connection timeouts.

## Root Cause
As mentioned in the issue, this was related to the comment in `tests.mockserver.http_resources.UriResource.render()` - the mock server's handling of CONNECT requests can interfere with SSL/TLS handshakes, causing race conditions between timeouts and SSL errors.

## Solution
Modified the test to accept both valid timeout-related error types:
1. `error.TimeoutError` - clean timeout (original expected behavior)  
2. `ResponseNeverReceived` - SSL handshake fails during timeout (flaky case)

## Changes
- Added `ResponseNeverReceived` import
- Updated `pytest.raises` to accept both exception types
- Added conditional assertion for `TimeoutError` domain checking
- Comprehensive comments explaining the dual error scenarios

## Testing
- ✅ Test now runs consistently (10/10 passes)
- ✅ All proxy-related tests still pass  
- ✅ No regressions introduced
- ✅ Proper error handling for both timeout scenarios

This maintains the test's intent while eliminating the flaky behavior.

prs.title: Setup Turbopack for development
prs.body:
This PR resolves the Turbopack setup issue by adding proper configuration and fixing hydration errors that occurred when using the `--turbo` flag.

## Changes

**Turbopack Configuration**
- Added experimental Turbopack config to `next.config.mjs` with MDX support
- Added path aliases for better module resolution  
- Added new scripts: `dev:turbo` and `next-dev-turbo`

**Hydration Fixes**
- IconLink: Fixed `window.matchMedia` SSR handling
- ContinueBtn: Added proper client-side state management for localStorage
- Monaco Editor: Used dynamic import to prevent SSR mismatches
- Chakra UI: Disabled `useSystemColorMode` to prevent color mode conflicts

**Documentation**
- Added `TURBOPACK_SETUP.md` with setup instructions and troubleshooting

## Testing
All tests pass (13/13). Both homepage and content pages work without hydration errors.

## Usage
```bash
# Turbopack (faster)
pnpm run dev:turbo

# Regular webpack
pnpm run dev
```

The main benefit is significantly faster build times while maintaining full compatibility with existing features.

Fixes the compilation and hydration errors mentioned in the original issue when using `next dev --turbo`.

prs.title: fix: add FUNDING.yml to ls-lint ignore list

- FUNDING.yml failed ls-lint validation due to uppercase letters
- GitHub requires FUNDING.yml to be uppercase (special config file)
- Added ignore pattern to allow FUNDING.yml while maintaining naming conventions for other YAML files
- Fixes issue #223

prs.title: [Go SDK] Add Write transform to cross-language Bigtable package - fixes #35873
prs.body:
Fixes #35873

## Summary
This PR implements a `Write` transform for the Go SDK's cross-language Bigtable package (`xlang/bigtableio`), bringing it to feature parity with the Bigtable I/O connectors in other Beam SDKs and providing a fully supported mechanism for writing data to Google Cloud Bigtable from Go pipelines.

## Changes Made
- Implement `Write` function with cross-language support using Java `BigtableIO.Write`
- Add `WriteOption` and `WriteExpansionAddr` for configuration
- Use `beam:schematransform:org.apache.beam:bigtable_write:v1` URN
- Follow same patterns as BigQuery xlang implementation
- Reuse existing expansion service infrastructure
- Update package documentation to include Write transform
- Brings Go SDK to feature parity with other Beam SDKs for Bigtable writes

## Problem Solved
Currently, Go developers who need to write to Bigtable are directed to use the community-supported `go/pkg/beam/io/bigtableio` package, which has limitations such as its internal use of `GroupByKey` on a global window that can cause streaming pipelines to stall indefinitely.

This implementation provides a fully supported cross-language alternative that leverages the robust Java `BigtableIO.Write` transform, offering the same reliability and features available to Java developers.

## Testing
- Package builds successfully
- All imports resolve correctly  
- Implementation follows established xlang patterns
- Consistent with existing BigQuery xlang implementation

## Usage Example
```go
import "github.com/apache/beam/sdks/v2/go/pkg/beam/io/xlang/bigtableio"

// Write rows to Bigtable
rows := beam.CreateList(s, []bigtableio.Row{sampleRow})
bigtableio.Write(s, "project-id", "instance-id", "table-id", rows)

// With custom expansion service
bigtableio.Write(s, "project-id", "instance-id", "table-id", rows,
    bigtableio.WriteExpansionAddr("localhost:8080"))

## Summary
Adds a "Year Filter Order" parameter to the archived articles menu item configuration, allowing users to sort years in ascending (oldest first) or descending (newest first) order.

## Pull Request for Issue #45772

Resolves #45772

## Summary of Changes
- Added `filter_field_years` parameter to `/components/com_content/tmpl/archive/default.xml`
- Modified `ArchiveModel::getYears()` to use the parameter with security validation
- Added language strings for the new parameter
- Backwards compatible (defaults to ASC - current behavior)

## Testing Instructions
1. Create an "Archived Articles" menu item
2. Go to Archive Options tab
3. Find "Year Filter Order" parameter
4. Set to "Most Recent First" 
5. View the archive page - years should show 2025, 2024, etc. (newest first)

## Types of changes
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [x] Backwards compatible

prs.title: [5.4] Add checkedout filter to the Article Manager
prs.body:
*Adapted by @muhme for the documentation and the test instructions for using -1 for checked out articles*

Pull Request for Issue #45724 .

### Summary of Changes
We can now filter articles by their checked out status using the API controller. This enhancement adds support for the checked_out filter parameter in the Content API (/api/index.php/v1/content/articles), allowing developers to filter articles based on whether they are checked out or not.

Changes made:
- Added `checked_out` filter support in `ArticlesController::displayList()` method
- The filter accepts integer values and maps them to the existing `filter.checked_out` model state
- Leverages the existing filtering logic already implemented in the front-end `ArticlesModel`


### Testing Instructions
Test 1: Filter for checked out articles only
``` GET /api/index.php/v1/content/articles?filter[checked_out]=-1 ```
- Expected: Returns only articles that are currently checked out by any user

Test 2: Filter for non-checked out articles only
``` GET /api/index.php/v1/content/articles?filter[checked_out]=0 ```

- Expected: Returns only articles that are not checked out

Test 3: Filter for articles checked out by specific user
``` GET /api/index.php/v1/content/articles?filter[checked_out]=123 ```
- Expected: Returns only articles checked out by user ID 123

Test 4: No filter parameter
``` GET /api/index.php/v1/content/articles ```
- Expected: Returns all articles regardless of checkout status (existing behavior preserved)

Test 5: Combined with other filters
``` GET /api/index.php/v1/content/articles?filter[checked_out]=0&filter[state]=-1 ```

- Expected: Returns only published articles that are not checked out


### Actual result BEFORE applying this Pull Request
The Content API did not support filtering articles by their checked out status. The `filter[checked_out]`  parameter was ignored, and there was no way to distinguish between checked out and available articles via the API.


### Expected result AFTER applying this Pull Request
The Content API now supports the `filter[checked_out]` parameter with the following behavior:

`filter[checked_out]=0` - Returns articles that are NOT checked out
`filter[checked_out]=-1` - Returns articles that ARE checked out
`filter[checked_out]=<user_id>` - Returns articles checked out by the specific user ID
No parameter - Returns all articles (maintains backward compatibility)

This enables API consumers to:

- Build custom admin interfaces showing article checkout status
- Implement workflows that depend on article availability
- Create dashboards displaying which articles are currently being edited
- Develop mobile apps that respect content locking mechanisms


### Link to documentations
Please select:
- [ ] Documentation link for docs.joomla.org: <link>
- [ ] No documentation changes for docs.joomla.org needed

- [ ] Pull Request link for manual.joomla.org: <link>
- [X] Will be added in https://manual.joomla.org/migrations/53-54/new-features with Beta 1

prs.title: [GH-1998] Fix typo in install-scala page (#1998)
prs.body:
## Did you read the Contributor Guide?

- [x] Yes, I have read the [Contributor Rules](https://sedona.apache.org/latest/community/rule/) and [Contributor Development Guide](https://sedona.apache.org/latest/community/develop/)
- [ ] No, I haven't read it.

## Is this PR related to a ticket?

- [ ] Yes, and the PR name follows the format `[SEDONA-XXX] my subject`.
- [x] Yes, and the PR name follows the format `[GH-XXX] my subject`.  
  (This addresses [GH-1998](https://github.com/apache/sedona/issues/1998))
- [ ] No:
  - [x] this is a documentation update. The PR name follows the format `[DOCS] my subject`
  - [ ] this is a CI update. The PR name follows the format `[CI] my subject`

## What changes were proposed in this PR?

This PR fixes a typo in the documentation page [`install-scala`](https://sedona.apache.org/1.5.0/setup/install-scala/), where the word **"user"** was mistakenly used instead of **"use"**.

As discussed in issue #1998, I’ve limited this PR only to the typo fix and did not proceed with mass `http` to `https` replacements, since it would affect too many files.

## How was this patch tested?

Since this is a markdown documentation fix, I manually verified the change in the file and ensured the typo is corrected.

## Did this PR include necessary documentation updates?

- [ ] Yes, I am adding a new API. I am using the [current SNAPSHOT version number](https://github.com/apache/sedona/blob/99239524f17389fc4ae9548ea88756f8ea538bb9/pom.xml#L29) in `vX.Y.Z` format.
- [ ] Yes, I have updated the documentation.
- [x] No, this PR does not affect any public API so no need to change the documentation.
